title: astragalus verus
intro: Astragalus verus, also known as milk vetch or white milk vetch, is a perennial plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This herb is believed to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which may help to boost the immune system and reduce stress. Astragalus verus has been traditionally used to promote health and well-being, with reported benefits including improved digestion, enhanced energy levels, and protection against respiratory infections and other illnesses. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its potential health benefits.
